observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that officials concerned particularly the Acting General Services
Officer and its local Bids and Award Committee personnel to regularly and to adhere to
proper internal processes by providing the resident auditor in advance with copies of the
Purchase Orders and within 24 hours after acceptance of such procurement inform the
assigned audit team leader of the deliveries made.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services
Officer to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE every year as
mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records
with the Accountant; and to create an inventory committee that will focus on the conduct
of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU.
Management mentioned during the exit conference that they are still not able to
form an inventory committee to address the Property, Plant and Equipment since the
assigned GSO has no assistant to conduct the inventory.
The audit team leader suggested that the LGU should have an inventory
committee per Office to facilitate the inventory taking and the assigned General Service
Officer to consolidate the report and conduct of ocular inspection to validate the
inventory. For Public Infrastructures the Local Chief Executive mentioned that they will
start and submit the inventory report.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management observe the general guidelines stipulated in
COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of
Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote Good
Governance through transparency and accountability.

Recommendation
We recommended that:
a. The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement
vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the
Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise,
we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other
emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the
Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2),P.D. 1445);
b. Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to maximize
work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload
and meet deadlines on reports submission; and
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing
Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the
same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time
prescribed by the regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management should require the municipal accountant and
municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in
accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the Auditor could
perform timely audit of the financial transactions of the agency.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Engineer must request earlier for inspection of
various Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation and support to the COA
Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical and Information Technology Services in
the inspection of said project by providing it with the complete project requirements and
guiding the COA engineers to the project sites. Likewise the MPDC should strictly
follow the guidelines stated in the DILG and DBM Circular in programming the 20%
Local Development Fund.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposits her collection regularly
as prescribed and to adopt necessary control measures in the handling deposits of cash
collections so as not to expose the scarce government monies to the risk of loss and
misuse by not depositing it intact to government depository banks as prescribed.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management makes a representation with Municipal
Accountant for the timely preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statement.

Recommendation:
We recommended that:
1. The Municipal Accountant should attach the computation of Terminal Leave of
the retirees for validation.
2. The Management strictly adhere to the above cited COA Circular and other
accounting rules and regulations, and
3. Direct the claimants to submit the above requirements to the COA Audit Team for
audit, verification, analysis and other audit actions.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that accountable officers must strictly adhere to the
COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any officials or
employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting thereof is
made. Furthermore, liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by
regulation.

We recommended to the management the following:
Compliance with pertinent laws and regulations on disbursements shall be strictly
observed. Completeness of supporting documents is necessary to establish correctness
and validity of the claim for payment.
The accounting office being the Internal Control Unit and in-charge in the
processing of money claims of the agency shall review and check well the audit.
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer failed to reconcile the
balances in the related accounts in the books of the Agency and the cashbook balance of
the Municipal Treasurer on quarterly basis leading to balance discrepancies due to timing
differences in the recording of transactions.

Recommendation:
The management shall prioritize registration with PhilGEPS not only to take
advantage of its built-in efficiencies but also avail of volume discounts inherent in bulk
purchasing. It may also promote transparency and efficiency in the procurement process
and give all interested parties an equal opportunity to bid.

We recommended that the LCE should:
a. ensure that there is an Approved Budget for SEF;
b. direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a separate depository
account for the SEF and regularly remit the SEF municipal share
thereof:
c. direct the Budget Officer to maintain a Registry for the SEF

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit
regularly and on time all required reports needed in order for us to validate and verify the
transactions.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE every year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records with the Accountant; and to create an inventory committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR- 2020 herein audit Part II- recommendation. Mgt. noted the
observations.reason: Audit observation and recommendation is reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended creation of the Municipal Inter-Agency Task Force COVID- 19 with its functions and mandate to strictly implement the approved policies and regulations of the national IATF – COVID 19 and strongly imposed its own localize guideline restrictions, medical protocols of incoming and out-going various travellers and to cushion the severe impact of eventual super-spreader and rise in COVID-19 infections among its constituents.

Providing Quarantine/Isolation facilities to address and confine suspected cases of COVID-19 infected individuals.

Immediate distribution of cash and in-kind assistance to its affected constituent during imposed community-wide lockdown to alleviate them from such difficult situation of making a living and putting foods on their tables. And such other similar activities to stop the spread and community transmission of such deadly COVID-19 threat.
observations.managementAction: AAR- 2020 herein audit Part II- recommendation. Mgt. noted the

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials of the Municipality of Indanan to support all claims with sufficient and required documentations, particularly, on the utilization of the COVID 19 appropriated funds in their procurement of medical related goods and services. Likewise, management must strictly observe laws, rules and regulations as provided for in Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on proper and sufficient required documentations and Section 52.1 of RA 9184 on Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee shall comply with the regulations on procurement through shopping. Procurement above the threshold should be done thru procurement service or public bidding pursuant to the provisions under RA 9184 to foster transparency and competitiveness and ensure the prices are the most advantageous to the municipality. However, splitting by dividing the projects into artificial phases to circumvent the regulations should not be resorted to. Stop the payment of purchases out of reimbursement basis unless circumstances warrant its application. Required documents on procurement shall be complete, properly numbered, signed and dated.
observations.managementAction: Mgt. noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that thru the Municipal Engineer’s Office in coordination with the BAC Chairman; facilitate the timely submission of all required documents associated to infrastructure projects implemented by administration. Submit the same to the office of the Audit Team Leader for verification, review and enabling for a timely request for technical inspection and validation by the COA-TAS ARMM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2),P.D. 1445); Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.
observations.managementAction: Mgt. noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.
observations.managementAction: Mgt. noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit regularly and on time all required reports needed in order for us to validate and verify the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Mgt. noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare a property crafted GAD Plan and provide us a copy of the GAD Accomplishment Report on a timely manner to provide us a basis in identifying whether these are aligned with the GAD Plan in accordance with the related guidelines, and to ensure full utilization of the budget allocation. Strict adherence with provisions under Joint Circular 2011-01 hereby enjoined.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management the following:
a. Compliance with pertinent laws and regulations on disbursements shall be strictly observed. Completeness of supporting documents is necessary to establish correctness and validity of the claim for payment.
b. The accounting office being the Internal Control Unit and in-charge in the processing of money claims of the agency shall review and check well the required documents before forwarding to the agency head for approval then finally to the Municipal Treasurer’s office for check preparation. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer should refrain from paying or releasing the check for disbursements with incomplete supporting documents.
c. The Municipal Accountant shall see to it that Report of Checks Issued and Report of Disbursements submitted by the Municipal Treasurer were supported with complete disbursement vouchers together with its supporting documents, and liquidation documents, respectively, before forwarding the same to the office of auditor for necessary examination and audit.
observations.managementAction: Mgt. noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their respective balances and to record the necessary adjustments to check the discrepancies in their record.
observations.managementAction: Mgt. noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: Strictly comply with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and Require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10) days after the award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the projects/ programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards and /or public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office.
observations.managementAction: Mgt. noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE Should: Ensure that there is an Approved Budget for SEF. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a separate depository account for the SEF and regularly remit the SEF municipal share thereof and direct the Budget Officer to maintain a Registry for the SEF
observations.managementAction: Mgt. noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposits her collection
regularly as prescribed and to adopt necessary control measures in the handling
deposits of cash collections so as not to expose the scarce government monies to
the risk of loss and misuse by not depositing it intact to government depository
banks as prescribed.

We recommended that management makes a representation with
Municipal Accountant for the timely preparation of the BRS.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal General
Services Officer to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE
every year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and
reconcile the records with the Accountant; and to create an inventory committee
that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the
LGU.

We have recommended that accountable officers must strictly adhere to
the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any
officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper
accounting thereof is made. Furthermore, liquidation of cash advances should be
made as required by regulation.

We recommended to the management the following:
 Compliance with pertinent laws and regulations on disbursements shall be
strictly observed. Completeness of supporting documents is necessary to
establish correctness and validity of the claim for payment.
 The accounting office being the Internal Control Unit and in-charge in the
processing of money claims of the agency shall review and check well the
required documents before forwarding to the agency head for approval
then finally to the Municipal Treasurer’s office for check preparation.
Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer should refrain from paying or releasing
the check for disbursements with incomplete supporting documents.
 The Municipal Accountant shall see to it that Report of Checks Issued and
Report of Disbursements submitted by the Municipal Treasurer were
supported with complete disbursement vouchers together with its
supporting documents, and liquidation documents, respectively, before
forwarding the same to the office of auditor for necessary examination and
audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer reconcile their respective balances and to record the necessary
adjustments to check the discrepancies in their record.

That the Bids and Awards Committee shall comply with the regulations on
procurement through shopping. Procurement above the threshold should be done
thru procurement service or public bidding pursuant to the provisions under RA
9184 to foster transparency and competitiveness and ensure the prices are the
most advantageous to the municipality. However, splitting by dividing the
projects into artificial phases to circumvent the regulations should not be resorted
to.

Stop the payment of purchases out of reimbursement basis unless
circumstances warrant its application.

Required documents on procurement shall be complete, properly
numbered, signed and dated.

We recommend that thru the Municipal Engineer’s Office in coordination
with the BAC Chairman; facilitate the timely submission of all required documents
associated to infrastructure projects implemented by administration. Submit the
same to the office of the Audit Team Leader for verification, review and enabling
for a timely request for technical inspection and validation by the COA-TAS
ARMM.

We have recommended that Management:
 Strictly comply with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No.
2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through
transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and
 Require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10)
days after the award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of
the projects/ programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects
signboards and /or public notices are already posted at the project site, for
proper validation by this Office.

We recommended that the:
 Require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the liquidation reports to
the source agency.

We recommended that the:
Concerned officials should fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered
by these fund transfers in order to accomplish the purpose for which the
funds were granted and deliver immediate and optimum result to target
beneficiaries;

We recommended that:
The management should instruct concerned officials to submit
disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline
set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month.
Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and
other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of
the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2),P.D. 1445);
Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to
maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work
overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions
and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting
Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and
change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid
Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an
arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in
order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results,
and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to
submit regularly and on time all required reports needed in order for us to validate
and verify the transactions.

We recommended that the LCE should:
Ensure that there is an Approved Budget for SEF. Direct the Municipal
Treasurer to maintain a separate depository account for the SEF and regularly
remit the SEF municipal share thereof and direct the Budget Officer to maintain a
Registry for the SEF

We recommended that Management direct the GAD Focal Person to
prepare a property crafted GAD Plan and provide us a copy of the GAD Accomplishment
Report on a timely manner to provide us a basis in identifying whether these are
aligned with the GAD Plan in accordance with the related guidelines, and to
ensure full utilization of the budget allocation.Strict adherence with provisions
under Joint Circular 2011-01 hereby enjoined.

properties of the LGU.

We recommended the Accountable Officer liquidate cash advance
within the prescribed period and submit report thereon to the Municipal
Accountant for recording in the journals and books of account, and
subsequently turn over to the auditor not later than the fifth day of the
ensuing month for necessary examination and audit immediately their cash
advances. All cash advances should be liquidated within the reglementary
period in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10,
1997 and Section 100 of P.D. 1445. Accountable Officer shall prepare
Report of Disbursement (ROD) per cash advance to enable us to identify
specific expenses paid out of that particular cash advance.

We hereby recommended the following:

That the Bids and Awards Committee shall comply with the
regulations on procurement through shopping. Procurement above
the threshold should be done thru procurement service or public
bidding pursuant to the provisions under RA 9184 to foster
transparency and competitiveness and ensure the prices are the most
advantageous to the municipality. However, splitting by dividing the
projects into artificial phases to circumvent the regulations should not
be resorted to.
Stop the payment of purchases out of reimbursement basis unless
circumstances warrant its application.
Required documents on procurement shall be complete, properly
numbered, signed and dated.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer reconcile their respective balances and to record the
necessary adjustments to check the discrepancies in their record.

We recommend that thru the Municipal Engineer’s Office in
coordination with the BAC Chairman, facilitate the timely submission of
all required documents associated to infrastructure projects implemented
by administration. Submit the same to the office of the Audit Team Leader
for verification, review and enabling for a timely request for technical
inspection and validation by the COA-TAS ARMM.

We have recommended that Management:

a). Strictly comply with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No.
2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through
transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and
b). Require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10)
days after the award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of
the projects/ programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects
signboards and /or public notices are already posted at the project site, for
proper validation by this Office.

We recommended that the:
a. Concerned officials should fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered
by these fund transfers in order to accomplish the purpose for which the
funds were granted and deliver immediate and optimum result to target
beneficiaries; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the liquidation reports to
the source agency.

We recommended to the management the following:
 Compliance with pertinent laws and regulations on
disbursements shall be strictly observed. Completeness of
supporting documents is necessary to establish correctness and
validity of the claim for payment.
 The accounting office being the Internal Control Unit and in-
charge in the processing of money claims of the agency shall
review and check well the required documents before
forwarding to the agency head for approval then finally to the
Municipal Treasurer’s office for check preparation. Likewise,
the Municipal Treasurer should refrain from paying or releasing
the check for disbursements with incomplete supporting documents.
The Municipal Accountant shall see to it that Report of Checks
Issued and Report of Disbursements submitted by the Municipal Treasurer
were supported with complete disbursement vouchers together with its
supporting documents, and liquidation documents, respectively, before
forwarding the same to the office of auditor for necessary examination and
audit.

We recommended that:
a. The management should instruct concerned officials to submit
disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to
meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is
every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise, we will be
compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other
emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the
requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied
with (section 122(2),P.D. 1445);
b. Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting
section to maximize work assignment among accounting
personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines
on reports submission; and
c. Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting
Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly
reports of their transactions and to submit the same together
with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section
within the time prescribed by the regulations.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management be formalize
and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and
doable Solid Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation
Plan. Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a
comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring
and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible
person.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO
to submit regularly and on time all required reports needed in order for us
to validate and verify the transactions.

We recommended that the LCE should:
a. ensure that there is an Approved Budget for SEF;
b. direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a separate depository
account for the SEF and regularly remit the SEF municipal share
thereof:
c. direct the Budget Officer to maintain a Registry for the SEF

We recommended that Management direct the GAD Focal Person to
prepare a property crafted GAD Plan and provide us a copy of the GAD
Accomplishment Report on a timely manner to provide us a basis in
identifying whether these are aligned with the GAD Plan in accordance
with the related guidelines, and to ensure full utilization of the budget
allocation.Strict adherence with provisions under Joint Circular 2011-01
hereby enjoined.

observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein  audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposits her collection regularly as prescribed and to adopt necessary control measures in the handling deposits of cash collections so as not to expose the scarce government monies to the risk of loss and misuse by not depositing it intact to government depository banks as prescribed.
We recommended that management makes a representation with Municipal Accountant for the timely preparation of the BRS.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE every year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records with the Accountant; and to create an inventory committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU.

We hereby recommended the following:
That the Bids and Awards Committee shall comply with the regulations on procurement through shopping. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the person(s) involved to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the documents enumerated in the preceding paragraph pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 and RA 9184. Moreover, as a control of the LGU, we enjoin the LCE to desist from approving the payment of contractual obligations without first ensuring that the contract documents have been submitted and received by the Audit Team.
The BAC explained during the exit conference that the Contracts are submitted to the Accounting Office. We reminded them the contract (and all their supporting documents) should be directly submitted to the Audit Team within 5 days from its perfection, and mere submission to Accounting Office is not acceptable. They assured us that they will now strictly follow the timely submission of the contracts. Meanwhile, they will submit all the contracts stated above not later than March 31, 2017.

The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.
